preamble: The shift from the bilabial nasal [m] to the voiced labiodental fricative [v] is a hallmark of Celtic lenition. It involves the weakening of the bilabial nasal closure into a fricative constriction, eventually losing its nasality to become a voiced fricative.

examples.note: The spelling 'mh' represents the historical lenited m.
Soft mutation (treiglad meddal) shifts m to f [v].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
